title: 2010 St Helens R.F.C. season

{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2024}} {{Use British English|date=October 2013}} {{ Infobox rugby league team season | year = 2010 | team_colour = #ffffff | font_colour = #ee0000 | league = Super League | league_link = [[Super League XV]] | league_rank = ''2nd'' | league_wins = 20 | league_draws = 0 | league_losses = 7 | points_for = 946 | points_against = 547 | playoff_result = ''Runners Up'' | cup = Challenge Cup | cup_result = ''Semi-finals'' | cup_wins = 3 | cup_losses = 1 | CEO_title = Chairman | CEO = Eamonn McManus | coach_title = Head coach | coach = [[Mick Potter]] | captain = [[Keiron Cunningham]] | stadium = [[Knowsley Road|GPW Recruitment Stadium]] | avg_attendance = 13,273 | high_attendance = 17,500
([[Warrington Wolves]], 19 March 2010)
([[Wigan Warriors]], 4 April 2010) | top_tries = [[Francis Meli]] (21) | top_goals = [[Kyle Eastmond]] (63) | top_points = [[Kyle Eastmond]] (188) | prev_season = [[2009 St Helens RLFC season|2009]] | next_season = [[2011 St Helens RLFC season|2011]] | list = yes | list_link = List of St Helens RLFC seasons | homejersey = St. Helens home jersey 2010.svg | awayjersey = }} '''[[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]''' entered their 136th year in [[rugby football|rugby]] in 2010, their 115th in [[rugby league]], and were in contention for the [[Super League]], in its [[Super League XV|15th Season]], and the 109th [[2010 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]]. ==League table== {{Super League XV ladder|showteam=STH}} ==Colours== St Helens stuck with tradition in selecting their home kit for 2010, with a white and red "vee" jersey, similar to the one worn by stars like [[Alex Murphy (rugby league)|Alex Murphy]] and [[Tom van Vollenhoven]] in the 1960s. The away kit was a striking, new colour combination never before seen by the Knowsley Road faithful, a sharp blue jersey with a white "vee" and red trim within the "vee". St Helens also launched a commemorative heritage strip that celebrates St Helens' 110th and final season at Knowsley Road. File:St. Helens home jersey 2010.svg|Home File:St. Helens heritage jersey 2010.svg|Heritage ==Pre-season== St Helens offset the loss of [[Jason Cayless]], [[Lee Gilmour]] and [[Sean Long]] with the signings of [[Rugby league positions#Prop|prop forward]] [[Nick Fozzard]] (returning to the club after a year at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]), [[Rugby league positions#Centre|centre]] [[Iosia Soliola]] from [[Sydney Roosters]] and [[Rugby league positions#Hooker|hooker]] [[Scott Moore (rugby league)|Scott Moore]] returning from loan spells at [[Castleford Tigers]] in [[Super League XIII|2008]] and [[Huddersfield Giants]] in [[Super League XIV|2009]]. The club also promoted youngsters [[Jack Bradbury (rugby league)|Jack Bradbury]], a centre, and [[Rugby league positions#Stand-off|stand-off]] [[Lee Gaskell]]. [[Matty Smith (rugby league)|Matty Smith]] and [[Steve Tyrer]] are again let out on loan, this time both players leaving for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] for the season, whilst [[Chris Dean (rugby league)|Chris Dean]] was "dual-registered" with [[Widnes Vikings]]. St Helens saw no coaching change despite finishing trophy-less for the first time in 4 years and some fans calling for the sacking of head coach [[Mick Potter]]. The club's pre-season went well, defeating Widnes and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] convincingly before the Under 21s fell to a 44–0 loss to [[Keighley Cougars]]. ==2010 results and fixtures== ===Friendlies=== ====Sunday 27 December==== '''St Helens''' vs. [[Wigan Warriors]] ''(off due to icy pitch)'' ====Tuesday 19 January ([[Vince Karalius|Karalius]] Cup)==== [[Widnes Vikings]] 26-42 '''St Helens''' ====Friday 22 January ([[Mike Bennett (rugby league)|Mike Bennett]] [[Testimonial match]])==== '''St Helens''' 38-18 [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] ====Sunday 24 January==== '''St Helens (Under 21s)''' 0-44 [[Keighley Cougars]] ===Super League XV=== {{see also|2010 Super League season}} ====Round 1==== '''St Helens''' vs [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] '''Preview:'''
The game will see former talismanic [[Rugby league positions#Scrum half|scrum-half]] and captain [[Sean Long]] return to [[Knowsley Road]] with his new side Hull; ironically as [[Rugby league positions#Captain|captain]]. The St Helens squad sees [[Francis Meli]] absent as the big [[New Zealand national rugby league team|Kiwi]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] [[Rugby league positions#Wing|wing]] is in his native [[Samoa]] on compassionate grounds. His place is likely to be taken by [[Gary Wheeler (rugby league)|Gary Wheeler]] or [[Jonny Lomax]]. Elsewhere, Saints are at full strength, with no place available for [[Rugby league positions#Second-row forward|second-rows]] [[Matty Ashurst]] or [[Andrew Dixon (rugby league)|Andy Dixon]] or [[Rugby league positions#Prop|prop]] Gareth Frodsham in the initial 19-man squad. '''St Helens''' 12-32 Hull St Helens' season got off to the worst possible start with a crushing loss to Hull at a foggy [[Knowsley Road]]. Despite a strong start, making good yardage and showing signs of the Saints flair of the [[Ian Millward|Millward]] and [[Daniel Anderson (rugby league)|Anderson]] eras, Saints failed to execute throughout and their defence proved leaky with [[Willie Manu]] cantering over in the 25th minute. [[Epalahame Lauaki]] barged over 12 minutes after brushing off [[Leon Pryce]], following more shocking defence from the five-time champions, and Hull continued to press after half-time, with [[Sean Long]] returning to haunt his former employers in a man-of-the-match performance, and [[Tom Briscoe]] adding scores to make the game safe with a 26–0 scoreline. Saints hit back through quick-fire tires from [[Matt Gidley]] and [[Chris Flannery (rugby league)|Chris Flannery]], but again, the finishing touch was found lacking in vital areas, meaning they could not pull off a comeback of old. [[Kirk Yeaman]] competed the win in the 75th minute after latching on to a [[Craig Fitzgibbon]] hack-on. No doubt, St Helens must improve on their basics should they want to trouble [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and usual pace-setters in the top four. {{Rugbyleaguebox | bg = #eeeeee | team1 = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] {{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} | team2 = {{leagueicon|Hull FC|16}} [[Hull F.C.]] | score = 12–32 | report = [http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217230 (Report)]| points1 = Tries: [[Matt Gidley]] 63'
[[Chris Flannery (rugby league)|Chris Flannery]] 69'

Goals: [[Danny Tickle]] (4/4)
[[Craig Fitzgibbon]] (2/2)| date = Saturday 6 February 2010{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/131|title=St Helens 12-32 Hull FC|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2010-02-06|accessdate=2010-02-14}} | stadium = [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside]]{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217230|title=St Helens 12-32 Hull FC|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2010-02-06|access-date=2010-02-08| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100210014530/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217230| archive-date= 10 February 2010 | url-status= live}} | attendance = 12,142 | referee = [[Ben Thaler]] | }} ====Round 2==== [[Bradford Bulls]] vs '''St Helens''' '''Preview:'''
The game sees St Helens' big [[Rugby league positions#Wing|winger]] [[Francis Meli]] return to the initial 19-man squad for the round 2 game at the [[Odsal Stadium]]. [[Rugby league positions#Second-row forward|second-row]] [[Matty Ashurst]] also comes back into contention for Saints. They take the places of [[Rugby league positions#Centre|centre]] [[Iosia Soliola]] who is out for three to four weeks with an [[ankle]] problem and wing [[Ade Gardner]] who has and [[intercostal muscle]] injury and is out of contention for six to eight weeks. Talismanic [[Rugby league positions#Hooker|hooker]] and [[Rugby league positions#Captain|skipper]] [[Keiron Cunningham]] was due to earn his 500th cap in the professional game but missed the fixture due to a family bereavement. Bradford Bulls 6-38 '''St Helens''' St Helens got back to winning ways after the week previous' crushing loss to Hull with a resounding win at Bradford. Saints started the game well, with [[Jon Wilkin]] opening the scoring in the 12th minute. From this St Helens kicked on, [[Kyle Eastmond]] kicking a penalty before [[Rugby league positions#Fullback|full-back]] [[Paul Wellens]] scored two of his eventual four tries before the half-hour mark. Saints continued the pressure after the half-time break with Eastmond intercepting and going 80 metres in the 48th minute. Bradford applied the pressure for fifteen minutes, but a mixture of awesome defence and poor decision making from the Bulls meant Saints kept them at nil. Wellens added his third and fourth in the 65th and 71st minutes before Bradford responded through a [[Matt Orford]] try and goal; a scant consolation score in the final moments of the game. A mention must go to [[Leon Pryce]] who had a hand in no less than 3 of his sides tries against his former employers. St Helens look to be back to their destructive best, and this win may relieve the pressure on under-fire [[Coach (sport)|coach]] [[Mick Potter]]. {{Rugbyleaguebox | bg = #eeeeee | team1 = [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] {{leagueicon|Bradford|16}} | team2 = {{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] | score = 6–38 | report = [http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=16580 (Report)]| points1 = Tries: [[Matt Orford]] 79'
Goals: [[Matt Orford]] (1/1) | points2 = Tries: [[Jon Wilkin]] 12'
[[Paul Wellens]] 19', 28', 65', 71'
[[Kyle Eastmond]] 48'
Goals: [[Kyle Eastmond]] (7/7)| date = Sunday 14 February 2010{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/2303|title=St Helens 38-6 Bradford Bulls|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2010-02-06|access-date=2010-02-14| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100329162107/http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/2303| archive-date= 29 March 2010 | url-status= live}} | stadium = [[Odsal Stadium]], [[Bradford]], [[West Yorkshire]]{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=16580|title=St Helens 38-6 Bradford Bulls|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2010-02-14|access-date=2010-02-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120829144254/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=16580|archive-date=29 August 2012|url-status=dead}} | attendance = 10,165 | referee = [[Phil Bentham]] | }} [[Catalans Dragons]] vs '''St Helens''' '''Preview:'''
St Helens will be looking to build upon last week's first win of the season at Bradford. However, they do not boast a healthy record at the [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]], winning only twice over four seasons. [[Andrew Dixon (rugby league)|Andy Dixon]] earns a recall, whilst [[Jack Bradbury (rugby league)|Jack Bradbury]] could make his St Helens début in the game after being named in his maiden 19-man squad. [[Chris Flannery (rugby league)|Chris Flannery]] misses out on the game through a shoulder injury, and he joins [[Iosia Soliola]] (ankle) and [[Ade Gardner]] (rib cartilage) on the injury list. [[Keiron Cunningham]] does not travel due to a family bereavement, as he still awaits his 500th game at professional level. Catalans Dragons 12-42 '''St Helens''' St Helens got back-to-back wins after a comfortable win over the Dragons in [[Perpignan]]. However, it was the Dragons who got the better of the opening exchanges, with [[Setaimata Sa]] and [[Chris Walker (rugby league)|Chris Walker]] going over inside the first ten minutes. Neither scores were converted, but Catalans had an 8–0 lead. However, on the 15 minute mark, Saints found their cutting edge and began to tick, with young winger [[Jonny Lomax]] latching on to an inch-perfect [[Paul Wellens]] chip. Despite [[James Graham (rugby league)|James Graham]] being off the field with suspected [[concussion]] and Jonny Lomax being caught by a dangerous tackle by [[Olivier Elima]] which resulted in the [[France national rugby league team|French]] [[Rugby league positions#Captain|captain's]] sending off, St Helens pressed on and it was Lomax, who shrugged off the shot from Elima to cut inside his opposing winger to score a brilliant try. [[Kyle Eastmond]] scored a goal from the touchline for a 10-8 St Helens lead, and with the extra man advantage, it was looking ominous for the Dragons. [[Francis Meli]] got his first of the season after a fine kick form the in-form [[Leon Pryce]], and shortly after, [[Matthew Gidley|Matt Gidley]] cut back inside his winger to finish a fine move for the first of his two tries of the evening. Half-time – 8–18 to Saints. The second-half continued in a similar guise, with [[Maurie Fa'asavalu]] scoring a trade-mark, barnstorming try under the posts. Eastmond was next to score, Gidley cutting through the defence and flicking the ball back on the inside to Eastmond who sped 30 meters for a fine try. Meli then crossed for his second, finishing well a break from [[Matty Ashurst]]. Gidley then got his brace, with a trademark, show and go on the outside to score. That was the end of the St Helens scoring, but by no means the end of the action, with [[Bryn Hargreaves]] and [[Scott Moore (rugby league)|Scott Moore]] involved in a brawl with the Catalans forwards. Walker got his second with five minutes remaining, but the win was in the bag by the hour mark. {{Rugbyleaguebox | bg = #eeeeee | team1 = [[Catalans Dragons]] {{leagueicon|Catalans|16}} | team2 = {{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] | score = 12–42 | report = [http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217245 (Report)]| points1 = Tries: [[Setaimata Sa]] 2'
[[Chris Walker (rugby league)|Chris Walker]] 9', 75' Goals: [[William Barthau]] (0/3) | points2 = Tries: [[Jonny Lomax]] 15', 24'
[[Francis Meli]] 29', 51'
[[Matthew Gidley|Matt Gidley]] 35', 59'
[[Maurie Fa'asavalu]] 44'
[[Kyle Eastmond]] 48'
Goals: [[Kyle Eastmond]] (5/8)| date = Saturday 20 February 2010{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/2314|title=Saints 42-12 Catalans|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2010-02-20|access-date=2010-02-21| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100329165501/http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/2314| archive-date= 29 March 2010 | url-status= live}} | stadium = [[Stade Aimé Giral]], [[Perpignan]], [[Pyrénées-Orientales]], France | attendance = 7,825 | referee = [[Steve Ganson]] | }} ====Round 4==== '''St Helens''' vs [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] '''Preview:'''
The game will St Helens' talismanic skipper Keiron Cunningham return to the fold to make his 500th appearance in professional rugby, following the sudden death of his mother, Annie. However, winger Ade Gardner and centre Sia Soliola still miss out through rib and ankle injuries respectively. Also, second rowers Jon Wilkin and Chris Flannery will both miss the game with shoulder problems, therefore meaning Matty Ashurst will deputise for his first appearance of 2010. '''St Helens''' 22-16 Wakefield Trinity Wildcats St Helens made it three consecutive wins with a hard-fought if scrappy win over the in-form Wakefield. Despite a strong, assured start from the Drednaughts with tries from [[Daryl Millard]] and young [[Dale Morton]] inside the first ten minutes, St Helens finally got going and got onto the scoreboard on 27 minutes with Jonny Lomax notching his third in four games. They continued to apply the pressure in the second half with scores from Kyle Eastmond, Francis Meli and Keiron Cunningham scoring on this landmark game for the hooker. Wakefield applied pressure late on with Millard getting his second, but St Helens held on for the win. ====Round 6==== St Helens 37 Crusaders 30 ==2010 transfers== ===Acquisitions=== {| class="wikitable" width="65%" style="font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=11 | Acquisitions |- !
